mission.title: bank-vaults: operator: Filter vault-operator cache to reduce memory usage
mission.description:
| Q               | A
| --------------- | ---
| Bug fix?        | yes
| New feature?    | no
| API breaks?     | no
| Deprecations?   | no
| Related tickets | fixes #1288 
| License         | Apache 2.0

### What's in this PR?
Upgrade controller-runtime to latest release (v0.11.2) and filter cache to only target those resources created by the controller/labeled with common keys. `app.kubernetes.io/name in (vault, vault-configurator)`

### Why?
In clusters with large number of configmaps, vault-o

steps.title: Apply the configuration
steps.description:
Apply the following configuration to your cluster:
```yaml
# k top pods -n keos-core vault-operator-86b8bf4596-gqpgr
NAME                              CPU(cores)   MEMORY(bytes)   
vault-operator-86b8bf4596-gqpgr   85m          2091Mi
```

steps.title: Review the fix
steps.description: The fix was implemented in https://github.com/bank-vaults/bank-vaults/pull/1621. Review the changes to understand the solution.

steps.title: Verify the fix
steps.description: Confirm that the issue is resolved in your environment by testing the affected functionality.

resolution.summary:
| Q               | A
| --------------- | ---
| Bug fix?        | yes
| New feature?    | no
| API breaks?     | no
| Deprecations?   | no
| Related tickets | fixes #1581
| License         | Apache 2.0

### What's in this PR?
Convert `map[interface{}]interface{}` to `map[string]interface{}` before sending the config to Vault API.
That's because the config data can have a sub dict (like `provider_config` in JWT/OIDC).

Without this conversion, Vault API will retrun the following error:
```
json: unsupported type: map[interface {}]interface {}
```

### Why?
This issue has been already fixed in #1247 however, it was removed (unintentionally) in the refactoring of v1.5 which caused #1581.

### Additional context
